rulings.Case Title: DENNIS HILL et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, APRIL 3, 2008 LINE 1. DEFENDANT CHRYSLER, L.L.C.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. IT IS GRANTED AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION (ENTERPRISE LIABILITY) AND TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION (FALSE REPRESENTATIONS); NO OBJECTION HAVING BEEN MADE. IT IS GRANTED AS TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION (PRODUCTS LIABILITY). DEFENDANT IS NOT RESPONSIBLE FOR EXPOSURES CAUSED BY REPLACEMENT PARTS OF OTHERS. DENIED AS TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES. IT APPEARS THAT DEFENDANT HAS SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF BASED ON PLAINTIFF'S DISCOVERY RESPONSE. ASSUMING THAT IS TRUE, THE CASTLEMAN DECLARATION CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: DENNIS HILL et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, APRIL 3, 2008 LINE 1. DEFENDANT TRANE U.S., INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS OFF CALENDAR. ORDER GRANTING JUDGMENT PER EX PARTE STIPULATION AND ORDER ENTERED ON MARCH 28, 2008. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: ABDULLAH SHAIKH VS. EQUINOX FITNESS CLUB OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, APRIL 3, 2008 LINE 2. DEFENDANT EQUINOX PINE STREET, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS CONTINUED TO APRIL 23, 2008. THE COURT REQUIRES THAT THE MOVING PARTY IS TO PROVIDE THE COURT WITH A SET OF COURTESY COPIES OF THEIR PAPERS. WHEN SUBMITTING THE DUPLICATE COPIES, PLEASE PROVIDE A COVER LETTER TO THE COURT REFLECTING THE NEW HEARING DATE SO THAT THE COURTESY COPIES WON'T BE MISPLACED WHEN RECEIVED. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: NORMAN DORNBLASER et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, APRIL 3, 2008 LINE 3. DEFENDANTS AMERICAN INTERNATIONAL INDUSTRIES, ARYZ CORP., ERX CORP., GLAMOUR INDUSTRIES, CO., RAZY PROPERTIES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. THE LOVELESS DECLARATION FAILS TO ESTABLISH THAT KLEER-VU AND NESLEMUR REMAINED VIABLE BUSINESSES AFTER THE ACQUISITION. SEE CHAKNOVA V WILBUR-ELLIS CO. (1999) 69 CAL. APP. 4th 962, 876-877. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE ARE SUSTAINED. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: SELLERS' REPRESENTATIVE, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. IRELAND SAN FILIPPO, L.L.P., A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, APRIL 3, 2008 LINE 7. DEFENDANT IRELAND SAN FILIPPO, L.L.P.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF ALLEGES IT SUFFERED A LOSS SEPARATE AND APART FROM ITS MEMBERS AND ITS MEMBERS HAVE ASSIGNED THEIR CLAIMS TO PLAINTIFF. ATLAS HOTEL IS DISTINGUISHABLE FROM THIS CASE FOR IT INVOLVED AN ACTION AGAINST THE INTERNAL REVENUE AND THE FEDERAL ASSIGNMENT OF CLAIMS ACT. THE MOTION TO STRIKE ATTORNEYS' FEES IS DENIED AT THIS EARLY STAGE OF THE LITIGATION. CAMENISHCH V SUPERIOR COURT (1996) 44 CAL. APP. 4TH 1689, 1699. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: CALDERA PHARMECEUTICALS, INC., A DELAWARE VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, APRIL 3, 2008 LINE 8. DEFENDANTS THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA AND LOS ALAMOS NATIONAL SECURITY, L.L.C.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE REGENTS WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS L.A.N.S. TO PLEAD FACTS THAT ARISE AFTER THE ASSIGNMENT. =(302/PJM/X-CL)

rulings.Case Title: CALDERA PHARMECEUTICALS, INC., A DELAWARE VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, APRIL 3, 2008 LINE 8. DEFENDANTS THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA AND LOS ALAMOS NATIONAL SECURITY, L.L.C.'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS GRANTED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD FACTS SUFFICIENT TO ESTABLISH A BASIS FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. DENIED AS TO PARAGRAPHS TWENTY AND TWENTY-ONE. THE COURT DECLINES TO TAKE JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE LICENSE AGREEMENT. =(302/PJM/X-CL)

rulings.Case Title: CALDERA PHARMECEUTICALS, INC., A DELAWARE VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To File Under Seal Portions Of A Document Submitted In Support Of Dfs' Mtn To Strike Portions Of Pl'S Complaint, Declaration
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, APRIL 3, 2008 LINE 8. DEFENDANTS THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA AND LOS ALAMOS NATIONAL SECURITY, L.L.C.'S MOTION To File Under Seal Portions Of A Document Submitted In Support Of DEFENDANT'S MOTION To Strike Portions Of PLAINTIFF'S Complaint IS GRANTED. MOVING PARTY IS IN COMPLIANCE WITH C.R.C. SECTION 2.551. =(302/PJM/X-CL)

rulings.Case Title: MAURA P. LOREDO VS. ALL AMERICAN FINANCIAL GROUP INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, APRIL 3, 2008 LINE 9. DEFENDANT OPTION ONE MORTGAGE CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AND OVERRULED IN PART. IT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE EIGHTH AND ELEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF ALLEGES THAT DEFENDANT IS AN ASSIGNEE WITH KNOWLEDGE AND/OR REASON TO KNOW. AS AN ASSIGNEE, DEFENDANT STANDS IN THE SHOES OF THE ASSIGNOR. THE ELEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION IS NOT SUBJECT TO CHALLENGE BY DEMURRER. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE TWELFTH CAUSE OF ACTION BASED ON BUSINESS AND PROFESSIONS CODE SECTION 17200. PLAINTIFF MISREADS COMMITTEE ON CHILDREN'S T.V. V GENERAL FOODS CORP. (1983) 35 CAL 3D, 197, 216-218. IT DOES NOT AUTHORIZE MINIMAL AND GENERAL DESCRIPTIONS OF FRAUD; IT ONLY REQUIRES LESS SPECIFICITY WHERE THE FACTS ARE KNOWN TO DEFENDANT. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE THIRTEENTH AND FOURTEENTH CAUSES OF ACTION BASED ON R.I.C.O. FOR FAILURE TO PLEAD A CRIMINAL ACT AND/OR HOW DEFENDANT'S ACTS ARE CRIMINAL OR SUBJECT TO INDICTMENT GERVASE V SUPERIOR COURT (1995) 31. CAL. APP. 4th 1218, 1230. =(302/PJM/ay)
